raw_input:
[5.0/5] Perfect for homemade antibacterial spray. So many uses! These were just what I needed to make my own alcohol antibacterial sprays. After being unable to find Lysol spray I resorted to mixing 1 part rubbing alcohol to 2 part water and adding to a spray bottle that ended up with a leak. I ended up trying these and it made 10 individual sized sprays that are perfect for on the go or putting one in every room if the house. I use these to spray groceries down, spray on cleaned countertops and faucets, etc. The plastic is thick and none have leaked. The set even came with two cute little funnels to help add your own liquids. There are so many uses for these and they are 2oz which is perfect for airline carry on bag requirements. I am so glad I saw these. Now, instead of having all of my antiseptic spray in one large bottle, I have 10 with the enclosed labels added, in each car and room of the house. [4.0/5] Thin cheap bottles not too expensive At about a dollar a piece for something that cost a few cents to make it's not the best deal in the world, but it's not bad. They work for my application - holding hand sanitizer - and hold an adequate amount. [5.0/5] They Work Great These perform very well. I use them to make my own essential oil (EO) bug sprays, after sun sprays, room refreshers, etc. They are small, spray well & don't leak.

The small size is great for portability. I take one of my bug spray formulas outside with me for while I work on my farm. Because I only use EO's the effectiveness wears off much faster than synthetic products. I keep the bottle on me to respray as needed. I have a bottle in my purse with a germ fighting blend for when stores are out of the cart wipes. I even made up a bottle of toilet spray. I use peppermint EO for that.

The funnel has a very narrow spout making pouring a slow processes but it keeps me from accidentally over filling the bottles. The tube that goes into the bottle is plenty long. It actually curls up in a C on the very bottom of the bottle. You can cut it if you want, but I leave it as is, I think they function better with the long tube. The stickers are a bit of a joke. I can't really fit "Bug Spray" on one tiny label. I'd have to write "BS" so I write "Bug" instead or "Sun" for example. It just barely fits.

Overall, great little spray bottles that suit my farm girl needs. [4.0/5] I got leaking inside the cap, but it didn't come out. This is a nice spray bottle kit, it comes with 2 funnels and a sheet of labels, I found the labels too small for use, each one is approximately 1 inch x 1/2 inch in diameter, and not sure if it can be used in an inkjet printer or not. All 10 spray bottles included all the necessary parts, and the stem that draws up liquid was long in each one, none were short and I really appreciated that. You should be able to spray down to the last drop. I tested mine with rubbing alcohol, for sanitizing my phones. The funnel came in handy for filling the bottle. It made the job so easy! The sprayer does spray a very fine mist. I used it several times to clean some things then left it overnight with the cap on. Next day I tested it again. First off, the cap contained some of the rubbing alcohol inside it, which means the container will leak into the cap standing up. Not necessarily a huge deal breaker, but a negative for me. Holding it in my hand I got the rubbing alcohol on my finger too, so this may waste product just from this. Several drops every 24 hours may not seem like a lot, but if you make your own essential oil room spray then this could be an expensive waste. Lastly, I had an issue with the spray top, the angle is so slight I wasn't sure if I would spray myself in the face of I wasn't wearing eyeglasses. You have to be careful, ensuring you're spraying away from yourself. I did not get any leakage of the alcohol out of the cap, it seemed pretty tight. It did pool in the cap, didn't run down the bottle. I did get a rubbing alcohol ring when I set down the cap on my coffee table tho. Beware of that possibility whatever you use in these bottles.
All in all, a nice kit and I give it 4 stars. [5.0/5] Love the size Great to use with water colors. A perfect size [5.0/5] Great Spray Bottle great mist coming out from this spray bottle and it has clear see through so i know what is the content and it even came with a label so i can name it as well. Thanks [4.0/5] Wash them well before using 💀 A spray bottle is a spray bottle, right? 🤔 Lol, nope!

So, here we are... shopping on Amazon, presented with a thousand options for something as simple as a bottle that sprays. 😰

My selection criteria was this: (a) The plastic must be able to handle liquid sanitizer made with a base of 60% alcohol (the 91% ISP variety), *without suffering undesirable changes* and (b) Have a good spray nozzle capable of producing a fine, even mist *without getting clogged*.

This set of bottles from MOSTOF checks both of those boxes. ✔ I tested them specifically against 2 different types of liquid (not gel) sanitizer. Initially, I chose this particular set for the larger sized funnels that are also included, and the fact that it comes with (10) travel sized bottles.

None of my bottles leak and they spray a fine, even mist without leaving droplets. After seeing the spray function in action, I decided to use a couple of these for facial mists and essences. I don't know (yet) whether they'll spray every last drop, but the sprayer feed wraps all the way around the bottom of the bottle, which does look promising. 🤞 So far, every liquid I've put in them has been fine; the plastic didn't change and the sprays perform the same.

Ultimately, I had to dock a star from my rating because the bottles came filthy inside, 😬 every one of them is heavily scratched up, and one of the bottles I received doesn't sit evenly. ➡️(see photos 3&4: defect (warped) bottle is on the right)⬅️

PRO'S:
+ Contains everything you need to package liquids immediately
+ Includes larger sized funnels = quick flow in filling
+ Spray nozzles produce fine, even mist without leaving droplets
+ Sprayer feed goes all the way around the bottom of the bottle
+ No leaking from the bottle necks or spray nozzles
+ Spray nozzles have not clogged
+ Caps fit tightly
+ No adverse reactions to the plastic (re: high alcohol content)
+ Airline approved carry-on size
+ Excellent price for ten bottles (and two funnels!)
+ Convenient PRIME shipping
+ Bonus sheet of labels (they're small, but they work)

CONS:
- Bottles came really scratched up and very dirty
- One of the ten bottles I received doesn't sit evenly

SUMMARY:
Ultimately, I think there's some quality control issues that the manufacturer could improve upon. As long as you're not an Etsy seller, or someone that's gonna be gifting these, you may not mind the scratches or the possibility of uneven bottom(s). Provided you wash them out really good, they still do the job! 👍🏼 Should anything happen to change with these as time goes on, I will update this review. Thanks for reading! Stay safe. 😷 [5.0/5] Little Spray Bottles These are good little plastic spray bottles. They work; they spray liquid. You can probably think of plenty of uses for them. I got them because I like to make my own anti-stink spray (with essential oils) to keep in my gym bag and spritz my equipment before I put it away. (Used to be roller derby gear; now it's curling shoes.) If you want to make your own freshening spray, these little bottles are a good way to do it! [3.0/5] quantity but not quality pros: I like that so many bottles come in a pack and that it includes a funnel for easy transfer of your rubbing alcohol/ hand sanitizer spray into the travel bottles.

cons: However, they only work less than 50 percent of the time. Like when you spray, liquid does not come out with every spray. It is frustrating to attempt to spray the bottle when nothing comes out. Also, the lids don't have a secure fit [5.0/5] Great! These are cute, the bottles look the size that I expected them to be. I love how big the funnels are! And as you can see they came packaged well. I would recommend! [5.0/5] Great little bottles Nice little bottles; they don't leak and they work perfectly with the liquid sanitizer I'm using. Recommended. [5.0/5] Good size Perfect size and works well [5.0/5] Perfect to carry in purse or car I bought these to use for hand sanitizer I bought in a gallon jug. Now everyone in my household has what they need. They were easy to fill and the pump works with no problem! [5.0/5] Handy little spray bottles I got these little spray bottles to be filled with alcohol and essential oils so I could have them in my purse, car, front door, etc... and always have one at the ready to disinfect my hands. They have been great. They are made of thick plastic with a long inner tube so every last drop gets used. I've used all of them and none clog or have stopped working.
Very very pleased with them. [5.0/5] Nice quality I love that funnels come
With this package for easy filling. [5.0/5] These are great for my spray hand sanitizers! I started carrying spray hand sanitizers recently for my online store and these bottles are great. The 2 ounce size is perfect for keeping in your purse. I love the fact you get a little funnel to help with filling. I use the 2x2" labels on them for my product info. Great value for these bottles. [5.0/5] Good for making Lysol to go Used for disinfecting car and other areas. Nice fit in purse or pocket [4.0/5] Nice bottles I like the small size for hand sanitizer. The kind I bought was liquid so I need the spray heads. I wish the funnels were wider, you have to pour the liquid slowly to fill the bottle and not overflow the funnel. the labels are okay, they should be larger to write something on them. I would order these again if I need more [5.0/5] Exactly what I wanted. I filled these bottles with liquid hand sanitizer from a larger bottle. The mist sprays without any trouble and it has not leaked after using for over a week. I've been able to keep bottles in all the cars and my purse. It works great and was a good price.

response:
Customer reviews mostly positive. Many use bottles for hand sanitizer, disinfectant spray, essential oils. Most say bottles no leak, spray fine mist, thick plastic good. Long inner tube mean use every last drop. Funnels helpful for filling, labels small but usable. Good size for purse, car, airline carry-on.

Bad things: some bottles come dirty and scratched inside. One reviewer get leaking into cap, leave ring on table. Another say spray only work half time, lids not fit tight. Labels too small for full words.

Overall customers happy with value. Ten bottles plus two funnels good deal. Most recommend.
